signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Carry + Options
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.32 — Strait of Hormuz closure and US Navy escort resumption revive USD safe-haven demand, overriding the bull flattener regime (2s10s +42.6bp). 278bp SOFR-TONA carry advantage sustains the bid despite BoJ hike bias. Options market remains defensive with a -1.41 moderate put skew (25d RR), signaling institutional hedging against intervention as price holds near the 160.00 threshold. Carry dominates while VIX remains at 17.2 (normal volatility).

signals.title: AUD/NZD — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Event Risk
signals.text: AUD/NZD G8 spread at 0.952% as NZD laggard status (-0.718% 1W avg) meets AUD outperformance. NZD COT LF net short -15,435 (specs fading hawkishness) vs RBNZ hike risk priced for the 27 May meeting. If RBNZ signals a July hike as flagged by MUFG, a crowded spec short unwind targets 1.0800+. AUD/NZD retail at 93% short represents an extreme contrarian reversal risk if the RBNZ disappoints.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Premium + Macro Divergence
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1629 — IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.3% generates a +2.8pt VRP, signaling elevated hedging demand as Hormuz tensions revive USD demand. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.00% creates a +175bp carry advantage for USD, supported by a restrictive +1.09% US real rate vs -0.32% for EUR. CESI divergence (USD +24 vs EUR +5) confirms superior US data momentum, keeping EUR upside structurally limited.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Volatility + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7167 — COT LF net long +57,180 (74.5% long) vs retail 74% short creates a sharp institutional vs retail divergence. Extreme +19.5% VRP (IV 27.8% vs HV30 8.3%) indicates the options market is pricing significant tail risk despite the 84bp AONIA-SOFR carry advantage for AUD. High-conviction spec longs face liquidation risk if AUD/Unemployment (z=+2.60σ shock) triggers a shift in RBA hold bias.

signals.title: DXY/SPX — Correlation Break + Funding Stress
signals.text: DXY vs SPX correlation at -0.662 vs 252d norm of -0.339 (z=+1.78σ). The intensified negative correlation during a session where both DXY and SPX are marginally higher/flat suggests USD is functioning as a primary risk-off funding hedge amid Middle East maritime disruptions. Watch for DXY to break 100.00 if Strait of Hormuz tensions escalate further, potentially decoupling from equity performance.
